title: Package Design, Modules, Versions, Workspaces, and Dependency Provenance · Mastery Check
summary: A team must split a command and library, publish a stable module, test a two-module workspace, and review dependency changes. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a versioned multi-package module plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Organize commands, internal packages, public packages, names, imports, cycles, and documentation around cohesive APIs.
Interpret module, go, toolchain, require, replace, exclude, retract, and godebug directives without treating local overrides as releases.
Explain semantic import versioning, minimal version selection, module graphs, sums, proxies, and private-module boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Package Api Layout · changed case · Quiz Package Api Layout
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A two-module workspace proposes dependency v2.2.0, a local replace, toolchain go1.26.5, and a public v2 package path. Explain MVS, checksum and proxy evidence, workspace-only success, API naming, and release compatibility.

Which action demonstrates Package Api Layout without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Package Api Layout · changed case · Quiz Package Api Layout.
